judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are near-identical and strong on (a)-(c), but Report 1 uniquely recommends dropping the hardcoded webhook count in favor of pointing at the source, so it edges ahead.

report_1.evidence: new refund calls belong in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py`, as a new accessor function ... `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4` and `:14` — imports `chargekit` directly ... `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2` and `:9-13` — same pattern ... The one file that *does* follow the documented exception correctly is `medibill/backend/app/billing/errors.py:6-9`, which only imports the SDK's error classes

report_2.evidence: New refund calls should live in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py` ... `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4,14` — imports `chargekit` directly ... `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2,9` — imports `chargekit` directly ... The only other file that imports `chargekit` is `medibill/backend/app/billing/errors.py:6` ... this is the doc's explicitly documented exception ... and is not a provider *call*

report_1.evidence: The actual `InvoiceOut` dataclass in `medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22` has 6 fields: `id, tenant_id, amount_cents, currency, status, external_ref`. `external_ref` was added by migration `medibill/backend/migrations/0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`

report_2.evidence: The actual `InvoiceOut` dataclass (`backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22`) has a sixth field, `external_ref: str | None` ... This field was added in migration `0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`

report_1.evidence: This should happen now, as its own commit, before/independent of the refund feature — not bundled invisibly into the refund PR, and not deferred ... the same PR that adds the new `gateway.refund()` accessor ... must update `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` in that same commit

report_2.evidence: The doc should be corrected now, independent of the refund feature ... That's a pre-existing accuracy bug, not something that should wait on new work ... as part of the refund feature PR itself, update the doc again ... add those to the relevant sections in the same commit that ships the code

report_1.evidence: stop asserting a count at all — either drop the number and just state the list (letting `webhooks.py` be the actual source of truth for "how many"), or point readers directly at `medibill/backend/app/api/webhooks.py` rather than restating a number that will go stale again
